meta.notes: DRAFT of the ELW Morning Prayer (Matins) ordo, pending owner verification against a physical/PDF ELW pew edition. Structure follows the Daily Office ordo in docs/research/2026-07-liturgy-strategy/09-elca-occasion-services.md: Opening versicles -> Psalmody -> Reading + responsory -> Benedictus -> office hymn -> Prayers (Kyrie / Lord's Prayer / collect) -> Blessing. TEXT PROVENANCE per docs/research/2026-07-liturgy-strategy/10-pd-text-sourcing.md: this rite bundles the traditional PUBLIC-DOMAIN default text (1662 BCP versicles/Gloria Patri, KJV Benedictus, ancient threefold Kyrie, traditional 'Our Father' Lord's Prayer) as pd.* / elw.lords_prayer catalog keys. ELW's contemporary wording (ELLC canticle 'My soul proclaims', ELW's opening dialogue, ELW's responsory, the Collect of the Day) is COPYRIGHTED and is NOT bundled: it is left as [VAR]/[LIC] slots (proper_slot / rubric) that resolve via the church's Augsburg/S&S license. [PD]=bundled here; [VAR]=per-day (readings/psalm/collect); [LIC]=ELW wording resolved by entitlement. Element status: opening versicles=[PD], Gloria Patri=[PD], psalmody=[VAR], reading=[VAR], responsory=[LIC] (no PD default authored - shown as a rubric), Benedictus=[PD KJV], office hymn=[VAR], Kyrie=[PD ancient], Lord's Prayer=[PD traditional], collect=[VAR/LIC], blessing=[VAR/LIC].

blocks.note: Composite cover page from CongregationProfile (church name/address, cover image, service time, date). Identity/chrome, not liturgical text.

blocks.title: *OPENING VERSICLES
blocks.note: [PD] 1662/1928 BCP opening versicle (Ps 51:15). Traditional-language default; ELW's contemporary opening dialogue is [LIC], resolved via entitlement.

blocks.title: *GLORIA PATRI
blocks.note: [PD] traditional doxology, 1662 BCP form. In Lent the 'Alleluia' that traditionally follows is omitted (practice/rubric, handled by the seasonal-customs layer).

blocks.title: Psalm
blocks.note: [VAR] Appointed psalm(s) for the day (traditionally the morning psalms, e.g. Ps 95 Venite / Ps 63). Number and text from the day's appointment (S&S / lectionary).

blocks.title: READING
blocks.note: [VAR] Appointed lesson for the day. The response ('Here endeth the reading' / 'Thanks be to God') is a renderer-side inline literal.

blocks.title: RESPONSORY
blocks.note: [LIC] A responsory follows the reading. ELW's specific responsory wording is copyrighted and resolves via the church's Augsburg/S&S license; no PD default is authored here (a traditional responsory could be substituted by the church). Shown as a rubric so no ELW wording is bundled.
[PD] Benedictus, Luke 1:68-79, King James Version (public domain). The bundled traditional-language default; ELW's ELLC contemporary canticle is [LIC], resolved via entitlement.

blocks.title: *OFFICE HYMN
blocks.note: [VAR] Optional office hymn. render mode is per-document renderer-side (bulletin=title, large print=lyrics).

blocks.title: KYRIE
blocks.note: [PD] the ancient threefold Kyrie (public domain), said inline so no ELW/Setting-Two Kyrie wording is bundled into the office.

blocks.title: *LORD'S PRAYER
blocks.note: [PD] Reuses the existing catalog key elw.lords_prayer, whose constant is the traditional 'Our Father, who art in heaven' wording (public domain). Preceded by an inline bidding renderer-side.

blocks.title: COLLECT OF THE DAY
blocks.note: [VAR/LIC] The appointed collect from S&S / the ELW propers; resolves via entitlement. No PD fallback bundled (it is variable per day).

blocks.title: *BLESSING
blocks.note: [VAR/LIC] Concluding blessing/benedicamus; S&S/ELW wording resolves via entitlement. No PD fallback bundled.
